FIRST AGENT BASELINE. UC health-element cut April 6 2026 reduces ~730K new disabled claimants by ~£3,000/year; ~400K projected into poverty. EMP/ACT/EQU show downward pressure offset by strong statutory accountability infrastructure. Composite 60.6 (Functional, near Established boundary). Delta -4.9 below 5pt threshold — confirmation. Monitor UC implementation effects in subsequent cycles.

DATA INTEGRITY RESOLVED. Live index 25.0 confirmed as canonical (vs rotation-state baseline 35.5 — reconciliation needed). HRW April World Cup warning + ICE 66K detention high + Senate Feb 2026 third-country deportation report all in pattern with 25.0 Developing. Composite holds.

FIRST AGENT BASELINE. Magyar TISZA party landslide April 12; ICC reversal pledge April 24; HRW April 14 agenda for incoming government. Composite 31.3 (Developing). Delta +3.2 below 5pt threshold — confirmation. STRUCTURAL TRANSITION SIGNAL — mandatory re-queue 30 days after Magyar government formation.

Wham trial scheduled April 6 2026; verdict not yet publicly available. HRW April 5 intervention. CIVICUS pattern confirms continued silencing of dissent. Pattern consistent with established 73.3. Composite holds.

emergingRisks: May 9 UNMISS substantive renewal vote: The technical rollover only buys 9 days. South Sudan's humanitarian situation continues to deteriorate. The substantive vote will determine UNMISS mandate scope — any reduction (particularly in force-strength or protection-of-civilians provisions) would be a methodology-note event even at the floor.

OpenAI trial verdict (est. mid-May): A judicial finding on the breach-of-charitable-trust claim would be the first precedent of its kind for AI governance structures. Binary outcome for OpenAI's INT/SYS scores.

Anthropic Mythos breach remediation clock: Each cycle that passes without published access-control remediation strengthens the case for a standalone SYS/ACC downward anchor. Currently absorbed as a sub-5pt offset.

UK Universal Credit implementation effects: The April 6 effective date means the first welfare-impact data will emerge in May-June 2026. If IFS and DRUK projections materialize (400,000 into poverty), the next UK assessment could cross the Established/Functional boundary — the first G7-nation band downgrade in the pipeline's history.

xAI regulatory escalation: ICO, EU DSA, California AG, and Ofcom proceedings are all active simultaneously. Country bans (Malaysia, Indonesia) are documented. Floor designation is already applied; the risk is that no regulatory action will be expressible within the current methodology.
DeepMind/Google data integrity: rotation-state shows 76.4, live ai-labs.json shows 65. This divergence predates tonight's proposal and must be reconciled before the proposal can be correctly applied.

Oracle WARN Act litigation: Investigations in Washington and Missouri are active. If class actions follow, they will be material in-window events affecting multiple ACC and BND subdimensions. 30-day re-queue scheduled.
highlights: Military AI governance fracture at DeepMind/Google: The Pentagon classified AI deal — signed April 28 despite 580+ employee opposition including 100+ DeepMind researchers — is the most significant single governance event across all AI labs since Anthropic's prior Pentagon exclusion. It represents the explicit abandonment of the weapons-use pledge that had distinguished Google's AI governance posture. The Established → Functional band crossing is the most consequential proposed change in this cycle.

Myanmar floor cluster now a 6-entity recommendation: Tonight's assessor recommendation to formally floor-designate Myanmar would expand the cluster to 6 entities — 3 AI labs, 3 countries. The April 10 inauguration of Min Aung Hlaing formally closes the coup's "transitional" framing. The April 26 martial-law expansion across 60 townships is the in-window structural-escalation signal. The pattern is identical to Sudan and South Sudan: harm-by-design, no exit-criteria evidence, deteriorating trajectory.

Turkey's ECtHR record surfaces as the diagnostic anchor: The 432 cumulative freedom-of-expression violations since 2002 — the largest cumulative Article 10 tally for any Council of Europe member — is a 24-year structural non-compliance pattern, not a weekly event. Combined with the April in-window journalist arrests and EFJ condemnation, the published score of 32.8 overstates Turkey's current institutional standing by 10.3 points.

Oracle layoff method is a benchmark touchstone: The 6:00 AM email / immediate-access-revocation / pre-layoff-monitoring combination is, across all Fortune 500 entities assessed in this cycle, the most structurally inverted set of EMP and BND subdimension signals documented. It will serve as a reference anchor for future mass-layoff assessments.

Week-end trend: AI labs are now the primary governance battleground of this cycle. Three of the four proposals tonight involve AI-sector entities (DeepMind/Google, Myanmar tangential, Oracle F500). Five of the six floor-designated entities are either AI labs or countries directly affected by AI-enabled weapons systems.

sectorAlerts.alert: Google/DeepMind signed classified Pentagon AI deal (April 2026) despite 580+ employee protest. Deal allows Gemini models for 'any lawful government purpose' on classified networks. Anthropic's Pentagon exclusion was the precedent that opened the contract opportunity. Multiple AI companies simultaneously in governance transitions around military AI.

Mass AI-driven tech layoffs: Meta (10%/8,000 jobs, April 23), Microsoft (6%/~10,000 jobs, April 24), Oracle (18%/30,000 jobs March 31). Combined 50,000+ jobs eliminated citing AI automation. 92,000+ tech sector jobs lost in 2026 YTD. Labor welfare dimension for multiple F500 and AI-lab entities.

Multiple national governments implementing welfare and disability benefit cuts in April 2026: UK Universal Credit health element cut effective April 2026 (400,000 pushed into poverty). US immigration detention at historic high 66,000+. Australia anti-protest laws. France dissolving civil society organizations. Broad pattern of state welfare contraction.

Three active conflict zones with worsening humanitarian indicators in 14-day window: South Sudan (UNMISS mandate expiry today + famine risk), Sudan (4th year of war, 34M in need), Myanmar (Min Aung Hlaing inaugurated April 10, air strikes intensifying). All three have critical-band composites.

Floor-designated entity: xAI/Grok regulatory exposure intensifying — ICO formal investigation (February 2026), EU DSA investigation, California AG cease-and-desist, Ofcom investigation, country-level bans (Malaysia, Indonesia). No remediation or reform signals found. Exit criteria not met.
